result.summary: Found several visual layout issues, mainly horizontal clipping on the right side and fixed bottom navigation overlapping page content.

issues.description: Multiple elements are cut off at the right edge of the viewport, including the top category tab row and the third game card column. This makes the page look horizontally overflowing rather than fitting the mobile screen.
issues.suggestion: Review the mobile layout width rules. Ensure containers use max-width: 100%, avoid fixed pixel widths that exceed the viewport, and make horizontal carousels clearly scrollable with proper padding.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation bar overlaps the game grid content near the bottom of the screen, hiding parts of the game cards underneath it.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the scrollable content so the last rows are not covered by the fixed navigation.

issues.description: The 'Hot Games' category label is visibly truncated as 'Hot Ga...' at the right edge of the tab row.
issues.suggestion: Either allow the tab row to scroll with clear spacing, reduce tab widths, or fit fewer tabs per row so labels are not abruptly clipped.

issues.description: The selected 'Home' tab has an odd gray/blue shadow or smudge along its lower edge, which looks like a rendering artifact.
issues.suggestion: Check the active tab box-shadow, gradient, or background layering and simplify the styling to avoid the unintended smudge.

issues.description: Game cards in the rightmost column are only partially visible, including cropped artwork and labels, which can make the grid feel broken if this is not intended as a carousel.
issues.suggestion: On mobile, use a clear two-column grid or a properly implemented horizontal carousel with consistent item widths, gutters, and scroll behavior.
